title: Blend a within-lap ranking model with a calibrated classifier
summary: Pit decisions are relative within a lap: among the 20 drivers on that lap, only a few are near stopping.
rationale: Pit decisions are relative within a lap: among the 20 drivers on that lap, only a few are near stopping. A ranking objective can model that field-level competition, while calibration/stacking can turn diverse models into stabler probabilities.
implementation_hint: Train a LightGBM `rank_xendcg` or `lambdarank` model with query groups `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` and relevance `PitNextLap`, then blend its OOF predictions with CatBoost/GBDT classifier outputs. Calibrate the final blend on race-held-out OOF predictions using sigmoid or isotonic regression.
expected_effect: Adds model diversity and can improve ROC AUC even if the ranking model is not the single best standalone predictor.
risk: Ranking labels are very sparse and calibration can overfit on small folds; keep the blend simple and validate by race group.
